errors: The ELM implementation has several discrepancies with the CPG requirements.
The ELM implementation has several mismatches with the CPG requirements.

The ELM implementation has a mismatch in the age threshold. The CPG requires Age >= 15 years AND Age <= 65 years, while the ELM implementation uses Age >= 30 years. This is a significant difference in the inclusion criteria and will lead to incorrect screening recommendations. The time interval also needs to be verified to ensure it aligns with the CPG's 1 year lookback period.

The ELM implementation has a mismatch in the age threshold. The CPG states “Age >= 18 years”, while the ELM implementation specifies “Age >= 25 years”. This violates the inclusion criteria. Additionally, the “Has Recent Alcohol Screening” logic is overly restrictive. The CPG states “No screening needed if… a screening has been performed within the past 1 year with a result present”, implying that a screening *with a present result* should not trigger a new screening. The ELM logic simply checks if *any* screening exists within the past year, regardless of the result.
